title: Ottleya flexuosa
taxonomy: Ottleya flexuosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and is a member of the tribe Crotalarieae. The genus Ottleya is part of the larger group of legumes, which are characterized by their fruit and seed characteristics. Ottleya flexuosa is native to the Middle East and North Africa, where it can be found growing in dry regions. This plant is also known by other names, including Oxytropis flexuosa, which highlights the potential for classification and naming variations.

intro: Ottleya flexuosa, also known as Desert Rose or Adenium, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular remedy for wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
